## SUMMARY

Built the governance infrastructure that makes agentic systems enterprise-safe, not as a prototype, but in production: exit-code-2 enforcement that blocks tool execution before damage occurs, a 5-question admission gate with 80 logged gate decisions, and a field-to-doctrine feedback loop that converts every production incident into an enforced rule loaded at session start. Three upstream contributions to anthropics/claude-code (#44707, #57946, #44711) establish credibility at the tooling layer the FDE practice depends on. Founder of AWACS LLC; methodology codified into five public frameworks and a portable engagement model designed to survive handoff and replicate at scale.

---

## SELECTED PROJECT · NATIONAL CLOUD PROVIDER: MULTI-CLIENT ENTERPRISE ENGAGEMENT MODEL AT SCALE

Designed data protection architecture for ~50 customer environments across dedicated, hybrid, private, and public cloud at National Cloud Provider; technical lead on largest accounts, built multi-tenant managed backup product, established Level I/II/III promotional path. Led migration of 13 public cloud datacenters with zero data loss and no single point of failure in delivery methodology. Managing 50 different customer configurations, where no two were alike, is the background for why portability and governance are first-class concerns in my AI deployment work.

---

## SELECTED PROJECT · AI ENABLEMENT SESSIONS: 200+ ATTENDEES, SVP TO ENGINEERS

I delivered two AI enablement sessions to 200+ attendees (SVP through engineers) on AI-powered observability and specialist agent methodology, and translated agentic architecture into demos leadership could act on. First session: a live AI-powered observability demo showing LLM triage of 3,000+ backup jobs per week. Second session: specialist agent methodology, how to assign roles, structure handoffs, and constrain agent scope for production reliability. The goal was to close the gap between "AI is powerful" and "here is what to do Monday."

## EXPERIENCE

**AWACS LLC** | Raleigh, NC | 2024 – Present
*Founder & Principal AI Engineer*

- **Production methodology for embedding AI agents in regulated enterprise environments** where vendor docs are insufficient and field debugging is the only ground truth; five public frameworks, taught publicly with demo-able working models and talks to 250+ live audiences (awacs.ai/methodology, awacs.ai/talks).
- **Gated knowledge base:** every entry must pass a 5-question admission gate before it is admitted, then sorted into Class A/B/C trust tiers; 62 execution-validated entries, 80 logged admit/reject decisions, 31 automated pre-session checks.
- **Governance enforced by code, not prompts:** a hook-level layer blocks destructive operations via exit(2); every production incident becomes an enforced rule that loads in every future session, so the methodology survives handoff. Proven at scale in a 31-hour completely autonomous zero-trust multi-agent build (github.com/Dwink213/awacs-secure-data-consolidation-lab-workstations).

## UPSTREAM · ANTHROPICS/CLAUDE-CODE

My agentic system studied the Claude Code release notes, surfaced these issues, validated them under my direction, and filed them under my account:

- **#44707** Hook exit codes: exit(1) is silently non-blocking; only exit code 2 stops a PreToolUse operation. Undocumented; produces false confidence in enforcement architecture.
- **#57946** PreToolUse enforcement silently bypassed on Windows / Git Bash; hooks exit 49 with no output when python3 resolves to the Store stub. Labeled area:hooks area:security platform:windows.
- **#44711** Three CLI flags in --help missing from the docs (--brief, --file, --mcp-debug); confirmed in v2.1.92.

---

## TECHNICAL COMPETENCIES

**Agent governance:** Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks, exit(2) enforcement, governance across file-level rules, hook enforcement, and infrastructure constraints, 5-question admission gate, Class A/B/C trust tiers, multi-agent orchestration (Analyzer + Librarian pattern), circuit breakers, SHA-256 brake events, append-only JSONL decision logs
**LLM & agentic systems:** Azure OpenAI, prompt engineering, orchestration (memory, state, tool-calling), MCP servers, sandbox tooling, high-fidelity internal demos, rapid iteration, player-coach technical leadership, agentic pipelines, LLM evaluation, shadow testing, application integration patterns
**Enterprise deployment:** Azure Local (HCI), Azure Arc SSH, WDAC, Azure Functions, Key Vault, Managed Identity, Bicep, GraphQL API introspection, ServiceNow integration, enterprise auth patterns (cert-based SP, SAS rotation)
